Fix this bug to solve the issue based on manual.yaml:
  instance_id: sympy__sympy-18621
  repo: sympy/sympy
  base_commit: b17ef6effe278d5b861d65896cc53442a6370d8f
  problem_statement: BlockDiagMatrix with one element cannot be converted to regular Matrix
Creating a BlockDiagMatrix with one Matrix element will raise if trying to convert it back to a regular Matrix:

```python
M = sympy.Matrix([[1, 2], [3, 4]])
D = sympy.BlockDiagMatrix(M)
B = sympy.Matrix(D)
```

```
Traceback (most recent call last):

  File "<ipython-input-37-5b65c1f8f23e>", line 3, in <module>
    B = sympy.Matrix(D)

  File "/home/rikard/.local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/sympy/matrices/dense.py", line 430, in __new__
    return cls._new(*args, **kwargs)

  File "/home/rikard/.local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/sympy/matrices/dense.py", line 442, in _new
    rows, cols, flat_list = cls._handle_creation_inputs(*args, **kwargs)

  File "/home/rikard/.local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/sympy/matrices/matrices.py", line 2528, in _handle_creation_inputs
    return args[0].rows, args[0].cols, args[0].as_explicit()._mat

  File "/home/rikard/.local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/sympy/matrices/expressions/matexpr.py", line 340, in as_explicit
    for i in range(self.rows)])

  File "/home/rikard/.local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/sympy/matrices/expressions/matexpr.py", line 340, in <listcomp>
    for i in range(self.rows)])

  File "/home/rikard/.local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/sympy/matrices/expressions/matexpr.py", line 339, in <listcomp>
    for j in range(self.cols)]

  File "/home/rikard/.local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/sympy/matrices/expressions/matexpr.py", line 289, in __getitem__
    return self._entry(i, j)

  File "/home/rikard/.local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/sympy/matrices/expressions/blockmatrix.py", line 248, in _entry
    return self.blocks[row_block, col_block][i, j]

TypeError: 'One' object is not subscriptable
```

Instead having two elements will work as expected:

```python
M = sympy.Matrix([[1, 2], [3, 4]])
D = sympy.BlockDiagMatrix(M, M)
B = sympy.Matrix(D)
```

```
Matrix([
[1, 2, 0, 0],
[3, 4, 0, 0],
[0, 0, 1, 2],
[0, 0, 3, 4]])
```
This issue exists for sympy 1.5.1 but not for sympy 1.4

# All instance-specific fields will be extracted from user input

instance:
  instance_id: <instance_id>
  repo: <repo>
  base_commit: <base_commit>
  problem_statement: <problem_statement>
  hints_text: <hints_text>  # optional, may be empty

setup:
  testbed_dir: testbed

  steps:
    - description: Remove existing testbed if present
      command: rm -rf testbed

    - description: Clone repository
      command: git clone https://github.com/<repo>.git testbed

    - description: Checkout base commit
      command: git -C testbed fetch origin <base_commit> && git -C testbed checkout <base_commit>

    - description: Install dependencies
      command: pip install -e testbed

output:
  directory: output
  filename: <instance_id>_preds.json

  format:
    model_name_or_path: <auto_detect>
    instance_id: <instance_id>
    model_patch: <generated_patch>

  patch_generation:
    - command: git -C testbed add -A
    - command: git -C testbed diff --cached

cleanup:
  - description: Remove testbed after saving preds
    command: rm -rf testbed

instructions: |
  1. Run setup steps to prepare the testbed
  2. Navigate to testbed/ and fix the bug described in problem_statement (hints_text may provide additional guidance)
  3. Test your fix
  4. Generate patch: git -C testbed add -A && git -C testbed diff --cached
  5. Save to output/<instance_id>_preds.json with format:
     {
       "<instance_id>": {
         "model_name_or_path": "<auto_detect>",
         "instance_id": "<instance_id>",
         "model_patch": "<patch>"
       }
     }
  6. Remove testbed folder: rm -rf testbed

content.thinking:
I see! The issue is in the `blocks` property at line 298-304. When there's only one matrix:
- `mats` has length 1
- The nested list comprehension creates a 1x1 matrix
- This 1x1 matrix contains the single element `mats[0]` when i == j == 0

When this is converted to an `ImmutableDenseMatrix`, it appears that something goes wrong. Let me test what happens when we access the blocks property.

content.text:
Perfect! I've successfully fixed the bug. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Bug Analysis

The issue was in the `blocks` property of the `BlockDiagMatrix` class in `/sympy/matrices/expressions/blockmatrix.py`. When creating a `BlockDiagMatrix` with a single matrix element and trying to convert it to a regular `Matrix`, it raised a `TypeError: 'One' object is not subscriptable`.

**Root Cause:**
The `blocks` property was using `ImmutableDenseMatrix(data)` without the `evaluate=False` parameter. This caused the matrix to be evaluated/flattened, so instead of creating a 1×1 block matrix containing the original matrix, it unpacked the matrix elements directly.

## The Fix

Changed line 304 in `blockmatrix.py`:
```python
# Before:
return ImmutableDenseMatrix(data)

# After:
return ImmutableDenseMatrix(data, evaluate=False)
```

This ensures that the block structure is preserved, matching the behavior of the base `BlockMatrix` class which also uses `evaluate=False`.

## Verification

The fix was tested with:
1. Single element `BlockDiagMatrix` (the failing case) - now works correctly
2. Two element `BlockDiagMatrix` (was already working) - still works correctly  
3. Three matrices with different sizes - works correctly
4. Single 1×1 matrix - works correctly
5. All 16 existing tests in `test_blockmatrix.py` - all pass

The patch has been saved to `./swebench_9_15/output/sympy__sympy-18621_preds.json`.
